hazard.summary: Secondary rate limits triggered not by raw request volume but by execution shape: unbatched per-entity mutations, repeated reads, and bursty write patterns across multiple skills.
signals: secondary rate limits hit during PR review workflows
per-comment POST fallback consumes 5 points per mutation
thread replies and resolutions executed individually
GitHub requires 1s spacing between mutating requests
requests should be serialized, not concurrent
retry resilience added previously did not resolve issue
batch endpoints reduce many writes to a single request

constraints: Mutating operations must be batched wherever the API supports aggregation.
Per-entity mutation loops must not emit rapid bursts under shared API budgets.
Retry resilience must not be used as a substitute for reducing avoidable API pressure.
Shared API budgets must be treated as a global execution constraint, not per-skill.
verification: Verify inline review comments are sent via a single batch request.
Verify thread resolutions are batched via GraphQL aliases.
Verify mutating calls are spaced by at least 1 second and not concurrent.
Verify total mutation points per run drop materially after batching.
notes: Key insight: rate limits are often self-inflicted by execution shape. Systems can respect retry/backoff and still fail if they generate unnecessary mutation density under shared budgets.
